summary: PostgreSQL 18 current minor is 18.4 and support ends 2030-11-14.
PostgreSQL 19 Beta 2 is a beta release and is not recommended for production.
Python 3.14.6 was released as a stable maintenance release on 2026-06-10.
Node 24 Krypton is an LTS line.
Next.js 16.2 is a stable release.
Xcode 26.6 includes the Swift 6.3 compiler.
KGP 2.3.20-2.3.21 fully supports AGP only through 9.0.0; KGP 2.4.x through 9.1.0.
AGP 9.2.0 requires Gradle 9.4.1 and JDK 17.
AGP 9+ enables built-in Kotlin and does not require the kotlin-android plugin by default.
Compose BOM 2026.06.00 is a stable versioned platform example.
gpt-5.6-sol is a public model ID and supports v1/responses.
OpenAI recommends Responses API for new projects.
Responses can retain application state for 30 days by default; store false and approved ZDR alter retention behavior, while abuse monitoring is separate.
SKIP LOCKED suits queue-like consumers; deterministic ORDER BY is needed with LIMIT.
Transactional outbox avoids database dual-write but duplicate delivery requires idempotent consumers.
Next.js BFF capabilities are a public API layer and are not a full backend replacement.
Autogenerate produces candidate migrations that require review; checks do not replace real migration execution.
Docker service containers are available on Linux runners, not macOS runners.
Korean privacy law requires minimization, separate sensitive-data basis, processor safeguards, overseas-transfer conditions, policy disclosure, and data-subject rights.
The PIPC guide applies privacy-by-design risk controls to commercial generative AI APIs across collection, deployment, and rights handling.
AI-operated services must give advance AI notice and label generative AI outputs.
A free-product relationship connected to a review must be clearly disclosed near the endorsement.
Non-medical services must not present diagnosis, treatment, or medical efficacy as medical practice or advertising.
The local machine currently reports Node 26.4.0 and OpenJDK 26.0.1, not the target Node 24 and JDK 17.
